howItWorks.intro: This macrame cord calculator uses established formulas to provide accurate results.
rules: Cut Length = Per Strand × 2 (folded in half for lark's head)
Total Cord = Cut Length × (Number of Cords ÷ 2)
howItWorks.outro: Results are estimates based on standard formulas. Verify with current local data for your specific situation.

faq.question: How much cord do I need for macrame?
faq.answer: General rule: cut cords 3-7× the finished length, depending on knot density. Square knots use 4× length, spiral knots 3×, and dense patterns up to 7×. Always add 10-20% extra to be safe.

faq.question: Why do I fold the cord in half?
faq.answer: Most macrame projects start by folding cords in half and attaching them to a dowel or ring with a lark's head knot. This means each cut piece creates two working strands, so cut pieces at 2× the per-strand length.

faq.question: What size macrame cord should I use?
faq.answer: 3mm cord: delicate jewelry and small projects. 4-5mm: plant hangers and medium wall hangings. 6-8mm: large wall hangings and curtains. Single-strand cord is best for detailed knot work.

faq.question: How accurate is this calculator?
faq.answer: This calculator uses standard crafting and DIY formulas trusted by crafters and makers. Results are reliable estimates for planning purposes. For critical decisions, we recommend consulting a qualified professional to verify.

faq.question: What finished length should I enter?
faq.answer: Enter the most accurate finished length value you have available. If you're estimating, use a conservative figure. You can always run the calculator again with different values to see how changes affect the results.

sections.title: What Is the Macrame Cord Calculator?
sections.body: The Macrame Cord Calculator is a free online tool designed for crafters and makers who need quick, accurate calculations in the crafting and DIY space. By entering your finished length, number of working cords, primary knot type, you get instant results including length per strand, total cord needed, total in feet. No formulas to memorize, no spreadsheets to build — just enter your numbers and get the answer in seconds. Whether you're a beginner or experienced professional, this calculator saves you time and eliminates guesswork.

sections.title: Why This Calculation Matters
sections.body: Getting length per strand right can make the difference between success and costly mistakes. In crafting and DIY, small errors compound quickly. Manual calculations are error-prone and time-consuming, especially under pressure. This calculator applies proven formulas used by crafters and makers worldwide, giving you confidence that your numbers are correct. Use it to plan your projects with precision and avoid common pitfalls that trip up beginners.

sections.title: When Should You Use This Calculator?
sections.body: This tool is most useful when you know your finished length and need to find the right length per strand. It's also great for quick estimates before committing to a decision, and to double-check manual calculations or professional quotes, and when comparing different scenarios side by side. Bookmark this page and come back whenever you need a fast, reliable answer — the calculator is always free and requires no signup.

howToSteps.title: Enter Your Finished Length (inches)
howToSteps.description: Start by entering your finished length — this is the primary input for the calculation.

howToSteps.title: Fill In Additional Details
howToSteps.description: Complete the remaining fields: number of working cords, primary knot type, fringe/tassel length, cord price. Each value refines the calculation for greater accuracy.

howToSteps.title: Click Calculate
howToSteps.description: Hit the Calculate button to run the numbers. Results appear instantly below.

howToSteps.title: Review Your Results
howToSteps.description: Check your length per strand, total cord needed, total in feet. Use these figures to inform your next decision or compare against alternative scenarios.

tips: Double-check your finished length before calculating — even small input errors can significantly change your results.
Run the calculator with different values to compare scenarios and find the optimal approach for your situation.
Pay attention to both length per strand and total cord needed — they work together to give you the full picture.

If you're unsure about your cord price, start with a conservative estimate and adjust from there.
